name: Persona Cartridges
description:
You discover the existence of "Persona Cartridges", small data cards (eerily akin to Nintendo Switch game cards) that exist within all people that can be ejected, swapped out, and manipulated. These cartridges contain a person's memories, behaviors, personality, experiences, essence, and "soul."

What will you do with this knowledge?

. . .

Initial Message 1: Choose your own setting/story. This bot was originally envisioned as a sandbox, more of a concept than a concrete story.

Initial Message 2: MalePOV. You are the only male student assigned to an all-girls dormitory at a state-of-the-art university.

Initial Message 3: Cyberpunk cold open. A set-up for a cyberpunk story centered on these powers. The setting is quite conducive for Persona Cartridge use, I feel.

. . .

This is supposed to be setting-agnostic (modern was in mind, but sci-fi, fantasy, or superhero settings work too). Artificial cartridges can be created with synthetic personalities or archetypes, if desired. Hence the brainwashing/mind control tags.

This is also my first bot and I will probably be editing and tweaking it frequently. I plan on adding new scenarios. I am open to requests/suggestions.

Use a proxy, I use DeepSeek and it's great. Some minor details get mixed up, but I use the methods below to fix them.

. . .

CW: Possession, brainwashing, identity play/death, dubcon, possible noncon.

. . .

Here are some tips to enhance your experience while chatting with bots:

Edit or Delete Messages (The Big One): If the bot starts speaking for you, you can edit its message to correct the direction of the conversation. Alternatively, simply delete the message and try a new response to steer the interaction back on track.

Edit and Regen from Your Last Message: If you like the majority of a message but the bot gets one thing wrong, add an [OOC:] addendum to YOUR last message then regenerate the response. (i.e. The bot gives a good response, but mentions and frequently references info the character the bot is narrating should not know. Edit your message and add "[OOC: Mary Sue does not know about XYZ]" to the end of it and regenerate. This is a great way to fix things from my experience.)

Write Detailed Messages: Crafting longer, more descriptive messages can help prevent the bot from speaking for you. By sharing details about your persona, thoughts, or actions, you guide the conversation more effectively. This approach can sometimes work well.

Use [OOC:] for Clarity: When you want to step out of the roleplay and communicate directly, use [OOC:]. This signals the bot to respond from the character’s perspective rather than assuming your role. Again, this might help, but results can vary.

Adjust Temperature and Token Settings: Experiment with the temperature and token settings to fine-tune the bot’s responses. While it might be a bit technical, tweaking these values can influence ho
